# Microcycle — Week of 2026-02-23

**Program:** Stage-Ready Physique Prep (5-Day PPL–UL Hybrid) — Delt/Triceps Priority + Knee-Friendly Legs  
**Phase:** Phase 1 — Accumulation / Tissue & Volume Base (Week 1 of 4)  
**User:** David Park  

## Schedule
- **Mon:** Push (Chest + Triceps Long Head + Lateral Delt top-up) — LA Fitness  
- **Tue:** Pull (Back + Rear Delts Priority) — LA Fitness  
- **Wed:** Legs (Knee-Friendly Quad Emphasis + Hamstrings) — LA Fitness  
- **Thu:** Rest / Steps + Mobility (optional posing)  
- **Fri:** Upper (Back/Chest Balance + Delt Volume) — LA Fitness  
- **Sat:** Physique Specialization (Delts + Triceps + Posing Support) — LA Fitness  
- **Sun:** Rest

## Week Overview
Week 1 of the accumulation block: the goal is to **establish repeatable baseline loads** in a deficit while keeping most compounds around **RPE 7–8** and isolations pushing **RPE 8–9** (last set can touch RPE 9–10 if joints feel great). Knee management is priority on leg day: **controlled depth**, no deep lunge patterns, sleeves on, and keep execution clean over chasing load.

---

# Workout — Monday, February 23, 2026
**Focus:** Pressing strength maintenance + long-head triceps hypertrophy  
**Location:** LA Fitness  
**Duration:** ~85 minutes

## Warm-Up (10 minutes)
1. Incline treadmill walk: 5 minutes @ easy pace  
2. Cable external rotation (elbow at side): 2 × 12/side (light)  
3. Scap push-ups: 2 × 10  
4. Incline DB press-specific warm-up (see ramp sets below)

## Main Workout

### 1. Incline Dumbbell Press
**Target:** 4 × 6–10 @ RPE 7–9  
- **Set 1:** 50 lb DBs × 10 (warm-up)  
- **Set 2:** 70 lb DBs × 8 (warm-up)  
- **Set 3:** 85 lb DBs × 10 (RPE 7.5)  
- **Set 4:** 90 lb DBs × 9 (RPE 8)  
- **Set 5:** 90 lb DBs × 8 (RPE 8.5)  
- **Set 6:** 85 lb DBs × 9 (RPE 8.5)  
**Rest:** 2.5–3 minutes between working sets  
**Notes:** Slight arch, shoulder blades pinned; 2-sec controlled eccentric, drive up without bouncing.

### 2. Hammer Strength Chest Press
**Target:** 3 × 8–12 @ RPE 8  
- **Set 1:** 90 lb/side × 10 (warm-up)  
- **Set 2:** 140 lb/side × 10 (RPE 8)  
- **Set 3:** 140 lb/side × 9 (RPE 8.5)  
- **Set 4:** 130 lb/side × 11 (RPE 8.5)  
**Rest:** 2 minutes between working sets  
**Notes:** Stop 1–2 cm short of hard lockout to keep tension on pecs.

### 3. Cable Fly (mid-to-low path)
**Target:** 3 × 12–20 @ RPE 8–9  
- **Set 1:** 25 lb/side × 15 (RPE 8)  
- **Set 2:** 25 lb/side × 14 (RPE 8.5)  
- **Set 3:** 20 lb/side × 18 (RPE 9)  
**Rest:** 75 seconds between sets  
**Notes:** Slight forward lean; 1-sec squeeze; keep shoulders down/back.

### 4. Overhead Cable Triceps Extension (rope)
**Target:** 4 × 10–15 @ RPE 8–9  
- **Set 1:** 60 lb × 12 (RPE 8)  
- **Set 2:** 60 lb × 11 (RPE 8.5)  
- **Set 3:** 55 lb × 13 (RPE 9)  
- **Set 4:** 55 lb × 12 (RPE 9)  
**Rest:** 75–90 seconds between sets  
**Notes:** Elbows slightly in; reach long overhead to load the long head; avoid flaring ribs.

### 5. EZ-Bar Skull Crusher to Slight Incline
**Target:** 3 × 8–12 @ RPE 8  
- **Set 1:** EZ bar + 50 lb (≈70 total) × 12 (RPE 8)  
- **Set 2:** EZ bar + 50 lb (≈70 total) × 10 (RPE 8.5)  
- **Set 3:** EZ bar + 40 lb (≈60 total) × 12 (RPE 9)  
**Rest:** 90 seconds between sets  
**Notes:** Let the bar drift slightly behind head (not straight to forehead). If elbows feel cranky, swap to DB overhead extensions next week.

### 6. Cable Lateral Raise (single-arm)
**Target:** 3 × 12–20 @ RPE 8–9 (2-sec peak)  
- **Set 1:** 15 lb × 16/side (RPE 8)  
- **Set 2:** 15 lb × 14/side (RPE 8.5)  
- **Set 3:** 12.5 lb × 18/side (RPE 9)  
**Rest:** 45–60 seconds between sides/sets  
**Notes:** Slight forward lean; lead with elbow; 2-sec squeeze at top.

## Cool Down (6 minutes)
1. Doorway pec stretch: 30 sec/side  
2. Overhead triceps stretch: 30 sec/side  
3. Lat hang (assisted if needed): 2 × 20 sec  
4. Slow nasal breathing: 1 minute

## Notes
- This is your **baseline push day** for the phase—keep these movements consistent for Weeks 1–3.
- Total time: ~86 minutes (estimated 85).

---

# Workout — Tuesday, February 24, 2026
**Focus:** Lats/back density + rear delt volume/quality  
**Location:** LA Fitness  
**Duration:** ~90 minutes

## Warm-Up (10 minutes)
1. Row erg: 5 minutes easy  
2. Banded lat prayer stretch pulses: 10 reps  
3. Straight-arm pulldown (very light): 2 × 12  
4. Rear-delt cable fly (very light): 1 × 20

## Main Workout

### 1. Neutral-Grip Lat Pulldown
**Target:** 4 × 6–10 @ RPE 7–9  
- **Set 1:** 120 lb × 10 (warm-up)  
- **Set 2:** 150 lb × 8 (warm-up)  
- **Set 3:** 180 lb × 10 (RPE 8)  
- **Set 4:** 190 lb × 8 (RPE 8.5)  
- **Set 5:** 180 lb × 9 (RPE 8.5)  
- **Set 6:** 170 lb × 10 (RPE 8.5)  
**Rest:** 2–2.5 minutes between working sets  
**Notes:** Slight lean back, chest up; pull elbows to front pockets; pause 0.5 sec at bottom.

### 2. Chest-Supported Row (machine)
**Target:** 4 × 8–12 @ RPE 8  
- **Set 1:** 90 lb/side × 12 (warm-up)  
- **Set 2:** 140 lb/side × 12 (RPE 8)  
- **Set 3:** 140 lb/side × 10 (RPE 8.5)  
- **Set 4:** 130 lb/side × 12 (RPE 8.5)  
- **Set 5:** 120 lb/side × 12 (RPE 8.5)  
**Rest:** 2 minutes between working sets  
**Notes:** Don’t shrug; think “elbows back, shoulder blades down.”

### 3. 1-Arm Cable Lat Row (lat bias)
**Target:** 3 × 10–14/side @ RPE 8–9  
- **Set 1:** 60 lb × 12/side (RPE 8)  
- **Set 2:** 65 lb × 11/side (RPE 8.5)  
- **Set 3:** 60 lb × 13/side (RPE 9)  
**Rest:** 60–75 seconds between sides  
**Notes:** Slight torso rotation allowed; keep upper trap quiet.

### 4. Reverse Pec-Deck (Rear Delts)
**Target:** 4 × 12–20 @ RPE 8–9 (1–2 sec squeeze)  
- **Set 1:** 120 lb × 18 (RPE 8)  
- **Set 2:** 130 lb × 16 (RPE 8.5)  
- **Set 3:** 130 lb × 15 (RPE 9)  
- **Set 4:** 120 lb × 18 (RPE 9)  
**Rest:** 60–75 seconds between sets  
**Notes:** Hands neutral; elbows slightly bent; squeeze rear delts—not mid traps.

### 5. Cable Rear-Delt Row (elbows high/wide)
**Target:** 3 × 12–16 @ RPE 8–9  
- **Set 1:** 60 lb × 15 (RPE 8.5)  
- **Set 2:** 60 lb × 13 (RPE 9)  
- **Set 3:** 50 lb × 16 (RPE 9)  
**Rest:** 75 seconds between sets  
**Notes:** Pull to upper chest/neck line; keep ribs down.

### 6. Incline DB Curl
**Target:** 2 × 10–14 @ RPE 8  
- **Set 1:** 35 lb DBs × 12 (RPE 8)  
- **Set 2:** 35 lb DBs × 10 (RPE 8.5)  
**Rest:** 75 seconds between sets  
**Notes:** Full stretch, no shoulder roll-forward.

### 7. Rope Hammer Curl
**Target:** 2 × 12–15 @ RPE 8–9  
- **Set 1:** 60 lb × 14 (RPE 8.5)  
- **Set 2:** 60 lb × 12 (RPE 9)  
**Rest:** 60 seconds between sets

## Cool Down (6 minutes)
1. Lat stretch on cable post: 30 sec/side  
2. Rear delt stretch across body: 30 sec/side  
3. Thoracic extension over bench: 8 slow reps

## Notes
- Rear delts got the first big dose this week—aim for **clean squeezes**, not load PRs.
- Total time: ~92 minutes (estimated 90).

---

# Workout — Wednesday, February 25, 2026
**Focus:** Hard leg stimulus without provoking left knee  
**Location:** LA Fitness  
**Duration:** ~95 minutes

## Warm-Up (12 minutes)
1. Bike: 6 minutes easy  
2. Leg extension (very light, ROM check): 1 × 20 @ 40 lb  
3. Lying leg curl (very light): 1 × 15 @ 60 lb  
4. Bodyweight box squat to comfortable depth: 2 × 8  
5. Put on **knee sleeves** before hack/press work sets

## Main Workout

### 1. Hack Squat (controlled depth you own)
**Target:** 4 × 6–10 @ RPE 7–9  
- **Set 1:** 1 plate/side (45) × 10 (warm-up)  
- **Set 2:** 2 plates/side (90) × 8 (warm-up)  
- **Set 3:** 3 plates/side (135) × 8 (RPE 7.5)  
- **Set 4:** 3 plates/side (135) × 8 (RPE 8)  
- **Set 5:** 3 plates/side (135) × 7 (RPE 8.5)  
- **Set 6:** 2 plates + 35/side (125) × 9 (RPE 8.5)  
**Rest:** 3 minutes between working sets  
**Notes:** Keep heels planted; stop 1–2 inches above the depth where the left knee feels “pinchy.” Smooth reps only.

### 2. Leg Press (feet mid, shoulder-width)
**Target:** 3 × 10–15 @ RPE 8–9  
- **Set 1:** 4 plates/side (180) × 12 (warm-up)  
- **Set 2:** 6 plates/side (270) × 15 (RPE 8)  
- **Set 3:** 7 plates/side (315) × 12 (RPE 8.5)  
- **Set 4:** 6 plates/side (270) × 13 (RPE 9)  
**Rest:** 2.5 minutes between working sets  
**Notes:** Control the bottom; don’t “dive-bomb” into hip tuck.

### 3. Smith Romanian Deadlift
**Target:** 3 × 8–12 @ RPE 8  
- **Set 1:** 95 lb × 10 (warm-up)  
- **Set 2:** 135 lb × 8 (warm-up)  
- **Set 3:** 185 lb × 10 (RPE 8)  
- **Set 4:** 185 lb × 9 (RPE 8.5)  
- **Set 5:** 175 lb × 10 (RPE 8.5)  
**Rest:** 2–2.5 minutes between working sets  
**Notes:** Soft knees, hips back; 2-sec eccentric; stop at mid-shin with neutral spine.

### 4. Seated Leg Curl
**Target:** 3 × 10–15 @ RPE 8–9  
- **Set 1:** 130 lb × 12 (RPE 8)  
- **Set 2:** 140 lb × 11 (RPE 8.5)  
- **Set 3:** 140 lb × 10 (RPE 9)  
**Rest:** 75–90 seconds between sets  
**Notes:** Hard 1-sec squeeze every rep.

### 5. Bulgarian Split Squat (controlled, not deep)
**Target:** 2 × 8–12/side @ RPE 7–8  
- **Set 1:** 25 lb DBs × 10/side (RPE 7.5)  
- **Set 2:** 25 lb DBs × 9/side (RPE 8)  
**Rest:** 90 seconds between sides  
**Notes:** Shorter stance, torso slightly forward; limit depth to keep left knee calm.

### 6. Standing Calf Raise
**Target:** 3 × 8–12 @ RPE 8  
- **Set 1:** 180 lb × 12 (RPE 8)  
- **Set 2:** 200 lb × 10 (RPE 8.5)  
- **Set 3:** 200 lb × 9 (RPE 9)  
**Rest:** 60–75 seconds between sets  
**Notes:** 2-sec stretch at bottom, no bouncing.

### 7. Seated Calf Raise
**Target:** 2 × 12–20 @ RPE 8–9  
- **Set 1:** 90 lb × 16 (RPE 8.5)  
- **Set 2:** 90 lb × 14 (RPE 9)  
**Rest:** 60 seconds between sets

## Cool Down (7 minutes)
1. Quad stretch (standing): 30 sec/side  
2. Hamstring stretch: 30 sec/side  
3. Calf stretch on step: 30 sec/side  
4. Easy bike: 2 minutes to flush

## Notes
- Knee rule this week: **no pain spike during or after**. If soreness is >3/10 next day, we’ll trim one quad movement set next week.
- Total time: ~97 minutes (estimated 95).

---

# Workout — Friday, February 27, 2026
**Focus:** Upper body density, extra delts/rear delts  
**Location:** LA Fitness  
**Duration:** ~90 minutes

## Warm-Up (10 minutes)
1. Cable row (very light): 2 × 12 @ 70 lb  
2. Band pull-aparts: 2 × 15  
3. Incline press ramp sets (below)

## Main Workout

### 1. Smith Incline Press
**Target:** 3 × 6–10 @ RPE 7–9  
- **Set 1:** 95 lb × 10 (warm-up)  
- **Set 2:** 135 lb × 6 (warm-up)  
- **Set 3:** 185 lb × 10 (RPE 8)  
- **Set 4:** 195 lb × 8 (RPE 8.5)  
- **Set 5:** 185 lb × 9 (RPE 8.5)  
**Rest:** 2.5–3 minutes between working sets  
**Notes:** Touch high chest, no bounce; stop 1 rep short of grind.

### 2. Seated Cable Row (moderate grip)
**Target:** 3 × 8–12 @ RPE 8  
- **Set 1:** 160 lb × 12 (RPE 8)  
- **Set 2:** 170 lb × 10 (RPE 8.5)  
- **Set 3:** 160 lb × 11 (RPE 9)  
**Rest:** 2 minutes between sets  
**Notes:** Slight pause at torso; keep lumbar position fixed.

### 3. Lat-Focused Pulldown (bilateral)
**Target:** 3 × 10–14 @ RPE 8–9  
- **Set 1:** 150 lb × 12 (RPE 8)  
- **Set 2:** 160 lb × 11 (RPE 8.5)  
- **Set 3:** 150 lb × 12 (RPE 9)  
**Rest:** 90 seconds between sets

### 4. Machine Shoulder Press (controlled)
**Target:** 3 × 8–12 @ RPE 7–8  
- **Set 1:** 160 lb × 12 (RPE 7)  
- **Set 2:** 170 lb × 10 (RPE 7.5)  
- **Set 3:** 170 lb × 9 (RPE 8)  
**Rest:** 2 minutes between sets  
**Notes:** Keep ribs down; stop shy of ugly reps—this is supporting work, not a max.

### 5. DB Lateral Raise (slight forward lean)
**Target:** 4 × 12–20 @ RPE 8–9  
- **Set 1:** 30 lb DBs × 18 (RPE 8)  
- **Set 2:** 30 lb DBs × 16 (RPE 8.5)  
- **Set 3:** 30 lb DBs × 14 (RPE 9)  
- **Set 4:** 25 lb DBs × 18 (RPE 9)  
**Rest:** 60 seconds between sets  
**Notes:** Partial reps allowed only after full-ROM slows; keep traps quiet.

### 6. Face Pull (rear delt bias)
**Target:** 3 × 12–20 @ RPE 8–9  
- **Set 1:** 60 lb × 18 (RPE 8)  
- **Set 2:** 60 lb × 16 (RPE 8.5)  
- **Set 3:** 60 lb × 15 (RPE 9)  
**Rest:** 60 seconds between sets  
**Notes:** Pull to eyebrow level; externally rotate at the end.

### 7. Cable Fly (shortened partials at end)
**Target:** 2 × 12–20 @ RPE 9  
- **Set 1:** 20 lb/side × 16 + 6 short partials (RPE 9)  
- **Set 2:** 20 lb/side × 14 + 6 short partials (RPE 9.5)  
**Rest:** 60–75 seconds between sets

### 8. Optional Arms Superset (keep it crisp)
**Target:** 2 rounds
- **Rope Pressdown:** 70 lb × 12 (RPE 8.5), 70 lb × 11 (RPE 9)  
- **EZ Curl:** 70 lb × 10 (RPE 8), 70 lb × 9 (RPE 8.5)  
**Rest:** 60 seconds between movements, 60 seconds between rounds  
**Notes:** If elbows complain, swap EZ curl to cable curl next week.

## Cool Down (6 minutes)
1. Pec minor stretch on post: 30 sec/side  
2. Upper back/lat stretch: 30 sec/side  
3. Neck/upper trap gentle stretch: 20 sec/side

## Notes
- Big delt volume day—expect a strong pump. Keep shoulder press submax to protect recovery.
- Total time: ~91 minutes (estimated 90).

---

# Workout — Saturday, February 28, 2026
**Focus:** Priority muscles + pump/metabolic work + posing support  
**Location:** LA Fitness  
**Duration:** ~75 minutes lifting + 15–20 minutes posing

## Warm-Up (8 minutes)
1. Easy bike: 4 minutes  
2. Cable lateral raise (very light): 1 × 20/side @ 7.5 lb  
3. Band external rotation: 1 × 15/side  
4. Rope pressdown (light): 1 × 20 @ 40 lb

## Main Workout

### 1. Cable Lateral Raise (strict)
**Target:** 3 × 12–20/side @ RPE 8–9  
- **Set 1:** 15 lb × 18/side (RPE 8)  
- **Set 2:** 15 lb × 16/side (RPE 8.5)  
- **Set 3:** 12.5 lb × 20/side (RPE 9)  
**Rest:** 45–60 seconds between sides  
**Notes:** 2-sec eccentric; stop just below shoulder height to keep tension.

### 2. Machine Lateral Raise (heavier)
**Target:** 3 × 8–12 @ RPE 8  
- **Set 1:** 150 lb × 12 (RPE 8)  
- **Set 2:** 160 lb × 10 (RPE 8.5)  
- **Set 3:** 150 lb × 11 (RPE 9)  
**Rest:** 90 seconds between sets  
**Notes:** Drive elbows out; no torso swing.

### 3. Reverse Pec-Deck (rear delts)
**Target:** 3 × 12–20 @ RPE 8–9  
- **Set 1:** 120 lb × 18 (RPE 8)  
- **Set 2:** 130 lb × 16 (RPE 8.5)  
- **Set 3:** 130 lb × 14 (RPE 9)  
**Rest:** 60–75 seconds between sets

### 4. Rear-Delt Cable Fly (cross-body)
**Target:** 2 × 15–25/side @ RPE 9  
- **Set 1:** 12.5 lb × 22/side (RPE 9)  
- **Set 2:** 12.5 lb × 18/side (RPE 9.5)  
**Rest:** 45–60 seconds between sides  
**Notes:** Think “reach wide,” keep shoulder down.

### 5. Overhead Cable Triceps Extension (rope)
**Target:** 4 × 10–15 @ RPE 8–9  
- **Set 1:** 60 lb × 13 (RPE 8)  
- **Set 2:** 60 lb × 12 (RPE 8.5)  
- **Set 3:** 55 lb × 14 (RPE 9)  
- **Set 4:** 55 lb × 12 (RPE 9.5)  
**Rest:** 75–90 seconds between sets  
**Notes:** Same setup as Monday; chase a deeper stretch and cleaner lockout.

### 6. Dip Machine (upright, triceps)
**Target:** 3 × 8–12 @ RPE 8  
- **Set 1:** 200 lb × 12 (RPE 8)  
- **Set 2:** 210 lb × 10 (RPE 8.5)  
- **Set 3:** 200 lb × 10 (RPE 9)  
**Rest:** 2 minutes between sets  
**Notes:** Upright torso, elbows track under wrists; avoid excessive shoulder glide.

### 7. Serratus/Waist Control
**Target:** Controlled, not max effort  
- **Cable Crunch:** 110 lb × 12 (RPE 8), 110 lb × 11 (RPE 8.5)  
- **Vacuum Practice:** 4 sets × 15 seconds (rest 30–45 sec)  
**Rest:** 60 seconds between cable crunch sets

### 8. Posing Practice
**Target:** 15–20 minutes  
- Front relaxed: 5 × 20–30 sec holds  
- Back relaxed: 5 × 20–30 sec holds  
- Transitions + “walk on/settle”: 5 minutes easy practice  
**Notes:** Focus on clavicles up, lats flared without shrugging, tight waist.

## Cool Down (5 minutes)
1. Delt stretch (across body): 30 sec/side  
2. Triceps stretch overhead: 30 sec/side  
3. Easy walk: 2 minutes

## Notes
- This day should leave you **pumped, not wrecked**. If elbows/shoulders feel irritated, we’ll reduce dip machine volume before touching overhead extensions.
- Total time: ~93 minutes including posing (estimated 90–95).

---

## Weekly Summary
- **Push (Mon):** Baseline incline DB press established at **90s for 9/8 reps** (RPE ~8–8.5). Long-head triceps got high-quality overhead volume without needing grinders.  
- **Pull (Tue):** Strong rear-delt emphasis (reverse pec-deck + rear-delt rows) with solid pulldown work up to **190×8**.  
- **Legs (Wed):** Knee-friendly quad loading via hack + leg press; kept depth controlled and avoided deep lunge stress.  
- **Upper (Fri):** Big delt volume day (4 hard lateral raise sets) while keeping shoulder press at **RPE ≤8** to protect recovery in a deficit.  
- **Specialization (Sat):** Priority-muscle pump + posing practice—high frequency for lateral/rear delts and long-head triceps without systemic fatigue spillover.  
- **Constraint monitoring (left knee):** Plan stayed within knee-friendly patterns (hack/press/BSS controlled). Next week decision hinges on how the knee feels 24–48h post-leg day.  
- **Next week:** Use **double progression**—add **+1 rep per set** where you were below the top of the range at target RPE, and only add load if you cap the range cleanly (especially on incline DB, pulldown, hack squat, and overhead triceps extensions).

<Plan>## Header
**Program name:** Stage-Ready Physique Prep (5-Day PPL–UL Hybrid) — Delt/Triceps Priority + Knee-Friendly Legs  
**Program Owner:** GymText  
**User:** David Park  
**Goal:** NPC Men’s Physique contest prep — cut from ~185 → ~175 while improving conditioning and bringing up rear delts, lateral delts, triceps long head; maintain lean mass/strength; keep left knee healthy  
**Duration:** **Ongoing with phase cycling** (contest peak can be slotted once show date/weeks-out are confirmed)

---

## Program Philosophy
David is an advanced men’s physique competitor cutting in a deficit, so the program prioritizes **hypertrophy stimulus retention** (hard sets close to failure), **fatigue management**, and **joint-friendly exercise selection** to keep training productive while recovery resources are limited. A **5-day PPL–UL hybrid** fits his preference and supports high weekly volume (15–20 sets/muscle/week) while allowing **extra frequency/volume for rear delts, lateral delts, and triceps long head** via targeted “micro-dosing” across the week. Legs are trained hard using **knee-tolerant patterns** (hack/leg press/Smith/BSS with controlled ROM) while avoiding known irritants (heavy back squat loading and deep lunges). Progression is primarily **double progression with RPE targets** (load or reps week-to-week), with a planned deload every 4th week and modest volume reductions as conditioning/cardio demands increase.

---

# Phase 1: Accumulation / Tissue & Volume Base (Weeks 1–4)
**Intent:** Build/maintain muscle with high-quality volume, keep joints happy, establish repeatable performance baselines in a deficit.

### Weekly Pattern

**Day 1 — Push (Chest + Triceps Long Head + Lateral Delt top-up)**  
- **Focus:** Pressing strength maintenance + long-head triceps hypertrophy  
- **Volume:** ~18–24 working sets  
1) Incline DB Press: 3–4 × 6–10 @ RPE 7–9  
2) Hammer Strength Chest Press (or Smith Incline): 2–3 × 8–12 @ RPE 8  
3) Cable Fly (mid-to-low or pec-deck): 2–3 × 12–20 @ RPE 8–9  
4) **Overhead Cable Triceps Extension (rope)**: 4 × 10–15 @ RPE 8–9

## Phase 1 Progression Strategy
- **Rep targets & progression (double progression):**  
  - Keep the exercise fixed for the whole phase.  
  - If you hit the **top of the rep range on all sets** at the target RPE, add load next week (small jumps: 2.5–10 lb depending on movement).  
  - If fatigue is high in deficit: add **1 rep per set** before adding load.
- **Effort/RPE:**  
  - Compounds: mostly **RPE 7–9** (avoid frequent true failure).  
  - Isolation: can reach **RPE 9–10** on final set(s) if joints feel good.
- **Deload (Week 4):**  
  - Reduce sets by ~40–50% (keep 1–2 hard sets per movement), maintain load but cap at **RPE 6–7**.
- **Transition trigger to Phase 2:**  
  - After Week 4 deload, or earlier if performance drops for 2 consecutive weeks and soreness/joint stress rises.

# Phase 2: Intensification / Strength Retention + Efficient Volume (Weeks 5–8)
**Intent:** Maintain performance on key presses/rows/leg patterns while slightly lowering volume to match increased diet/cardio fatigue. Priority muscles stay high frequency.

### Weekly Pattern (same 5 days; slightly “tighter” volume)

**Day 1 — Push (Heavier top sets + long-head focus)**  
- Incline DB Press: 1 top set × 6–8 @ RPE 9, then 2 back-off × 8–10 @ RPE 8  
- Hammer Strength Press: 2 × 8–10 @ RPE 8  
- Cable Fly: 2 × 12–15 @ RPE 9  
- Overhead Cable Extension: 4 × 8–12 @ RPE 8–9  
- Lateral Raise (cable): 2 × 15–20 @ RPE 9

**Day 2 — Pull (Heavy row + rear delt volume)**  
- Pulldown: 3 × 6–10 @ RPE 8–9  
- Chest-Supported Row: 1 top set × 6–8 @ RPE 9 + 2 × 8–10 @ RPE 8  
- 1-Arm Cable Lat Row: 2 × 10–12 @ RPE 8–9  
- Reverse Pec-Deck: 4 × 12–20 @ RPE 9  
- Cable Rear-Delt Row: 2 × 12–16 @ RPE 9  
- Curls: 3 total sets

**Day 3 — Legs (Knee-friendly, slightly lower total sets)**  
- Hack Squat: 3 × 6–10 @ RPE 8–9  
- Leg Press: 2–3 × 10–15 @ RPE 8–9  
- Smith RDL: 3 × 6–10 @ RPE 8–9  
- Leg Curl: 3 × 10–15 @ RPE 9  
- Calves: 4–5 sets total

**Day 4 — Upper (Density + delts)**  
- Smith Incline Press: 3 × 6–10 @ RPE 8  
- Cable Row: 3 × 8–12 @ RPE 8–9  
- Pulldown: 2 × 10–14 @ RPE 9  
- Machine Shoulder Press: 2 × 8–10 @ RPE 8  
- DB Lateral Raise: 4 × 10–20 @ RPE 9  
- Face Pull / rear delt: 2 × 15–20 @ RPE 9

**Day 5 — Specialization (Delts/Tris pump + posing)**  
- Lateral raise variation A: 3 sets  
- Lateral raise variation B: 3 sets  
- Rear delt: 4–6 sets total  
- Overhead triceps: 4 sets  
- Pressdown/dip machine: 2–3 sets  
- Posing: 15–25 min

### Phase 2 Progression Strategy
- **Top set + back-off model:** aim to keep the top set load stable/increasing even while cutting.  
- **Weekly goal:** add **1 rep** to top set OR add a small load jump while keeping reps.  
- **Volume guardrails:** if sleep/recovery tanks, pull **2–4 sets/week** from non-priority muscles first (chest/back/arms), keep delts and long-head triceps more stable.  
- **Deload:** every 4th week (Week 8) or earlier if knee/elbow irritation emerges.

---

# Phase 3: Pre-Contest “Polish” / High Fatigue Management (Weeks 9–12)
**Intent:** Keep stimulus high but time-efficient; manage systemic fatigue; increase pump/posing emphasis. (This phase also works as a “mini-cut” block if show timing is unclear.)

### Weekly Pattern (5 days; shorter sessions, more supersets)
**Day 1 Push:** maintain incline strength + triceps overhead (reduced total pressing sets)  
**Day 2 Pull:** back maintenance + rear delts high  
**Day 3 Legs:** maintain quads/hams with knee-friendly choices; no grinders  
**Day 4 Upper:** pump/density, minimal joint stress  
**Day 5 Specialization:** delts + triceps + posing priority

**Key implementation rules**
- **Superset a non-competing isolation with compounds** (e.g., incline press ↔ lateral raises; rows ↔ rear delts) to keep intensity without extending sessions.
- **Isolation work can go to near-failure** (RPE 9–10) while compounds stay **RPE 7–8**.

### Phase 3 Progression Strategy
- **Progress by “quality” first:** better execution, longer controlled eccentrics, improved MMC, stable loads at same RPE.  
- **Load progression is optional**; rep progression is preferred.  
- **Deload:** every 4th week, or insert a “recovery micro-week” if weight loss accelerates and performance dips sharply.

---

## Phase Cycling
**Default cycle (non-date-specific):**  
**Phase 1 (Accumulation) → Phase 2 (Intensification) → Phase 3 (Polish) → repeat**, with deloads every 4th week.

**When to move phases**
- **Time-based:** 3 hard weeks + 1 deload week.  
- **State-based:** transition earlier if (a) performance regresses 2 weeks running, (b) joints flare (knee/elbow/shoulder), or (c) sleep/recovery drops and pumps flatten.

**If a confirmed show date exists (recommended once you confirm):**
- Run Phase 1 until ~12–10 weeks out  
- Phase 2 from ~10–5 weeks out  
- Phase 3 from ~5 weeks to peak week  
- Add a **taper** in the final 7–10 days: keep frequency, cut sets by ~30–50%, keep pumps, avoid soreness-inducing novelty.

- **Set 2:** 160 lb × 10 (RPE 8.5)  
- **Set 3:** 150 lb × 11 (RPE 9)  
**Rest:** 90 seconds between sets  
**Notes:** Drive elbows out; no torso swing.

### 3. Reverse Pec-Deck (rear delts)
**Target:** 3 × 12–20 @ RPE 8–9  
- **Set 1:** 120 lb × 18 (RPE 8)  
- **Set 2:** 130 lb × 16 (RPE 8.5)  
- **Set 3:** 130 lb × 14 (RPE 9)  
**Rest:** 60–75 seconds between sets

### 4. Rear-Delt Cable Fly (cross-body)
**Target:** 2 × 15–25/side @ RPE 9  
- **Set 1:** 12.5 lb × 22/side (RPE 9)  
- **Set 2:** 12.5 lb × 18/side (RPE 9.5)  
**Rest:** 45–60 seconds between sides  
**Notes:** Think “reach wide,” keep shoulder down.

### 5. Overhead Cable Triceps Extension (rope)
**Target:** 4 × 10–15 @ RPE 8–9  
- **Set 1:** 60 lb × 13 (RPE 8)  
- **Set 2:** 60 lb × 12 (RPE 8.5)  
- **Set 3:** 55 lb × 14 (RPE 9)  
- **Set 4:** 55 lb × 12 (RPE 9.5)  
**Rest:** 75–90 seconds between sets  
**Notes:** Same setup as Monday; chase a deeper stretch and cleaner lockout.

### 6. Dip Machine (upright, triceps)
**Target:** 3 × 8–12 @ RPE 8  
- **Set 1:** 200 lb × 12 (RPE 8)  
- **Set 2:** 210 lb × 10 (RPE 8.5)  
- **Set 3:** 200 lb × 10 (RPE 9)  
**Rest:** 2 minutes between sets  
**Notes:** Upright torso, elbows track under wrists; avoid excessive shoulder glide.

### 7. Serratus/Waist Control
**Target:** Controlled, not max effort  
- **Cable Crunch:** 110 lb × 12 (RPE 8), 110 lb × 11 (RPE 8.5)  
- **Vacuum Practice:** 4 sets × 15 seconds (rest 30–45 sec)  
**Rest:** 60 seconds between cable crunch sets

### 8. Posing Practice
**Target:** 15–20 minutes  
- Front relaxed: 5 × 20–30 sec holds  
- Back relaxed: 5 × 20–30 sec holds  
- Transitions + “walk on/settle”: 5 minutes easy practice  
**Notes:** Focus on clavicles up, lats flared without shrugging, tight waist.

## Cool Down (5 minutes)
1. Delt stretch (across body): 30 sec/side  
2. Triceps stretch overhead: 30 sec/side  
3. Easy walk: 2 minutes

## Notes
- This day should leave you **pumped, not wrecked**. If elbows/shoulders feel irritated, we’ll reduce dip machine volume before touching overhead extensions.
- Total time: ~93 minutes including posing (estimated 90–95).

---

## Weekly Summary
- **Push (Mon):** Baseline incline DB press established at **90s for 9/8 reps** (RPE ~8–8.5). Long-head triceps got high-quality overhead volume without needing grinders.  
- **Pull (Tue):** Strong rear-delt emphasis (reverse pec-deck + rear-delt rows) with solid pulldown work up to **190×8**.  
- **Legs (Wed):** Knee-friendly quad loading via hack + leg press; kept depth controlled and avoided deep lunge stress.  
- **Upper (Fri):** Big delt volume day (4 hard lateral raise sets) while keeping shoulder press at **RPE ≤8** to protect recovery in a deficit.  
- **Specialization (Sat):** Priority-muscle pump + posing practice—high frequency for lateral/rear delts and long-head triceps without systemic fatigue spillover.  
- **Constraint monitoring (left knee):** Plan stayed within knee-friendly patterns (hack/press/BSS controlled). Next week decision hinges on how the knee feels 24–48h post-leg day.  
- **Next week:** Use **double progression**—add **+1 rep per set** where you were below the top of the range at target RPE, and only add load if you cap the range cleanly (especially on incline DB, pulldown, hack squat, and overhead triceps extensions).</Week>
